account_plan_attestation: Starter paid subscription, stated by the account owner on 2026-07-14
commercial_use_basis: generated during the account owner's paid Starter subscription; use is subject to the ElevenLabs terms captured by the raw candidate metadata

cues.prompt: One isolated tactile game selection sound: a small carved hardwood chess piece tapped once by a gloved finger on a thick wooden chessboard. Close-miked dry studio Foley, crisp natural wood transient, tiny short resonance. Exactly one tap. No footsteps, no electronic beep, no music, no ambience, no voices, no reverb.

One isolated realistic medieval footstep: a heavy leather boot with a little plate armor plants once firmly on a solid wooden floor. Close-miked dry Foley, audible heel-to-sole impact, convincing leather and wood texture, very faint armor jingle, short decay. Exactly one footstep. No second step, no walking sequence, no music, no ambience, no voice, no cinematic boom, no synthetic tones.

One isolated realistic sword contact: a forged steel longsword blade strikes another steel blade in a hard medieval parry. Sharp physical metal-on-metal transient, solid impact body, brief irregular steel ring, close-miked dry Foley. Exactly one strike. No sword swing or whoosh before it, no repeated clashes, no explosion, no bass boom, no music, no crowd, no voices, no large hall reverb.

One isolated realistic armored fall: a human-sized medieval fighter in plate and leather collapses heavily onto a solid wooden floor. Deep body thud followed immediately by a short loose armor clatter and leather movement, close-miked dry Foley, natural decay. One fall only. No footsteps, no sword strike, no vocal grunt, no music, no ambience, no cinematic sub-bass, no long reverb.

A very short medieval battle-start herald cue played by two real natural trumpets: four bold notes, immediate confident attack, ceremonial but compact, ending cleanly. Isolated studio recording. No percussion, no orchestra, no crowd, no speech, no battle sounds, no long reverb, no modern synthesizer.

A concise triumphant medieval victory fanfare played by a small group of real herald trumpets: clear rising phrase, strong resolved final chord, about three seconds, celebratory and memorable. Isolated clean recording. No drums, no full orchestra, no crowd, no speech, no battle noise, no modern synthesizer, no long reverb tail.

One compact medieval danger warning for a chess check: three quick tense strikes on a small forged-steel signal bell, the middle strike slightly lower and the last resolving sharply. Dry close recording, urgent and unmistakable, clean short decay. No voice saying check, no music bed, no footsteps, no sword clash, no ambience, no long reverb.

mastering.description: onset trim, exact duration fit, equal-power mono downmix, DC removal, 24 Hz high-pass, short edge fades, and 4x reconstructed-peak gain staging

clap.purpose: candidate triage only; not a substitute for listening
clap.reproducibility: normalized candidate score arrays identical across two independent runs

selection.pilot_policy: retain the six user-auditioned move/capture/fall pilot candidates

sfx_sublicensing_opt_out: not observable through the API; managed with the Disable control on the ElevenLabs Sound Effects product page
standalone_distribution: intended only as bundled Chess Bash game assets, not a standalone SFX library

music.note: The three music tracks are locally generated by music_sound_generator, which drives Strudel (AGPL-3.0) headlessly and captures pure-synth patterns. The AUDIO is the output of running the tool, not Strudel source, and the patterns use only synthesised waveforms so no sample-bank licence applies. Output is NOT byte-deterministic (real-time capture); final_sha256 is as-rendered and the reproducible pin is pattern + pattern_sha256. Previous music archived under ~/research/gpu_terminal/sound/older_assets/chess-bash/music_originals_2026-07-16/. The 25 SFX cues above are unrelated (ElevenLabs).

tracks.tool: Strudel (https://strudel.cc), AGPL-3.0 — invoked as an external renderer
tracks.synthesis: pure synthesised waveforms (sine/sawtooth/triangle/square + noise); NO sample banks, so no sample licence applies

pattern.synthesis: synthesised only: s("sine"/"sawtooth"/"triangle"/"square") + noise ("white"/"pink"/"brown"); no sample banks
